stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the Meta-Llama-3-8B model, a significant LLM from Meta.
It is featured on Hugging Face Benchmarks, indicating its current popularity and performance tracking.
The associated tags (transformers, llama, text-generation) confirm its relevance to the core NLP/AI community.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It relates to core NLP/ML components, specifically text-generation pipelines and transformer architectures.
The mention of Llama indicates relevance to the highly competitive and significant LLM ecosystem.

stories.title: Google must let publishers opt out of AI Search features, rules UK
stories.author: Jess Weatherbed
bullet_points: UK regulator (CMA) has mandated that Google must allow publishers to opt out of its AI Search features.
This ruling directly impacts how content from online publishers is used in Google's AI Overviews and similar AI Search functionalities.
The decision represents a significant regulatory shift, giving content owners more control over their data usage in generative AI.

stories.title: Microsoft's Project Solara is an Android OS designed for agents instead of apps
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Microsoft is developing Project Solara, a new Android OS.
The OS is fundamentally designed around AI 'agents' rather than traditional applications.
This signals a potential industry shift away from app-centric mobile experiences toward agent-driven interaction.

stories.title: Humanoid-GPT: Scaling Data and Structure for Zero-Shot Motion Tracking
stories.author: Li Yi
bullet_points: Introduction of Humanoid-GPT, a GPT-style Transformer designed for whole-body control in humanoid robotics.
The model is trained on a massive, unified 2B-frame motion corpus, overcoming limitations of prior shallow tracking methods.
Scaling data and model capacity allows Humanoid-GPT to track highly dynamic behaviors with improved generalization.

stories.title: 1-Click GitHub Token Stealing via a VSCode Bug
stories.author: ammar2
bullet_points: A critical vulnerability exists in VSCode allowing for token theft.
The exploit enables attackers to steal GitHub tokens with a single click.
This represents a significant security risk to developers using VSCode and GitHub.

stories.title: Full Disclosure: 1-Click GitHub Token Stealing via a VSCode Bug
stories.author: blog.ammaraskar.com by ammar2
bullet_points: A critical vulnerability exists in VSCode that allows for the theft of GitHub tokens.
The exploit is described as a '1-Click' attack, indicating high ease of exploitation.
This represents a significant security risk targeting developers using VSCode and GitHub.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a specific model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, trending on Hugging Face.
The model is categorized for text-generation and conversational AI tasks.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key ML/NLP tags like 'transformers' and 'pytorch'.
The context suggests tracking the performance and trends of large language models.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for enhanced performance.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware, optimizing AI workloads for Apple devices.
The feature is currently available in a preview state, indicating ongoing development and optimization.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging apps.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs on the user's own device.
The core value proposition is bringing powerful, private AI capabilities to everyday communication tools.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, both locally and in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is collaborating with OpenAI and ROOST to integrate gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models.
These models are designed for safety classification tasks and are available in 20B and 120B sizes.
The models are released under the permissive Apache 2.0 license, promoting open usage.

stories.title: Amazon’s search bar will invent AI-generated products you can’t buy
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: Amazon is integrating AI into its search bar to generate visual representations of requested products.
The initial rollout is limited to AI-generated images for clothing and home goods.
Users can use these AI images as a visual prompt to search for actual, purchasable items.

stories.title: Amazon-owned Ring should pay Americans for scanning their faces, lawsuit says
stories.author: Jon Brodkin
bullet_points: Ring cameras, owned by Amazon, are reportedly using AI to scan and identify faces of guests and passersby.
A lawsuit alleges that Ring is collecting biometric data from Americans without proper compensation or consent.
The story highlights significant privacy and surveillance concerns surrounding consumer-grade smart home technology.

stories.title: Android phones will soon be able to detect spoofed calls and impersonation scams
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Google is integrating enhanced scam detection into the Android operating system.
The new feature specifically targets the detection of spoofed calls and impersonation scams.
This update is part of a broader Android feature drop that also includes more AirDrop and AI enhancements.

stories.title: Language Models Need Sleep: Learning to Self-Modify and Consolidate Memories
stories.author: Vahab Mirrokni
bullet_points: Addresses a key limitation of current LLMs: the inability to continually learn and transfer temporal in-context knowledge to long-term parameters.
Proposes a novel approach, inspired by human learning, to enable LLMs to 'self-modify and consolidate memories,' akin to sleep.
Represents research pushing beyond current instant prediction capabilities toward more robust, lifelong learning systems in AI.

stories.title: Agentic Chain-of-Thought Steering for Efficient and Controllable LLM Reasoning
stories.author: Julian McAuley
bullet_points: Introduces Agentic Chain-of-Thought Steering (ACTS) to improve LLM reasoning efficiency and controllability.
Addresses the inefficiency and lack of inference-time control in standard extended chain-of-thought reasoning.
Frames reasoning steering as a Markov decision process managed by a controller agent.

stories.title: Agentic Chain-of-Thought Steering for Efficient and Controllable LLM Reasoning
stories.author: Julian McAuley
bullet_points: Introduces Agentic Chain-of-Thought Steering (ACTS) to improve LLM reasoning efficiency and controllability.
Addresses the inefficiency and lack of inference-time control in standard extended chain-of-thought methods.
Formulates reasoning steering as a Markov decision process managed by a controller agent.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, offering an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The framework emphasizes local-first operation, allowing AI to run entirely on user hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, integrating local LLM execution.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has expanded its capabilities to support the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
The feature promotes the use of powerful proprietary models alongside open-source alternatives.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex functionality allows models to read, modify, and execute code locally.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives like gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: PlayStation is getting back to what it’s good at
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: PlayStation is shifting its development focus away from live-service games.
The company is prioritizing premium, narrative-driven, single-player experiences.
This strategic pivot was highlighted during the recent State of Play showcase.

stories.title: Microsoft and OpenAI broke up — now they’re ready to fight
stories.author: Tom Warren
bullet_points: Microsoft showcased significant new AI initiatives at its Build conference.
Announcements included a super app, in-house reasoning models, and new cybersecurity tools.
The news signals Microsoft's aggressive positioning as a major competitor in the AI landscape.

stories.title: AI has a water problem. Google thinks it has a fix
stories.author: Lauren Feiner
bullet_points: Google is addressing environmental backlash regarding AI data center water usage.
The company is committing to increasing water availability for local communities.
Google outlined five commitments in a new blog post aimed at minimizing water impact.

stories.title: Microsoft plans Linux tools and an RTX Spark desktop for Windows developers
stories.author: Andrew Cunningham
bullet_points: Microsoft is expanding its support for Linux tools within its ecosystem.
The company announced the RTX Spark desktop, targeting Windows developers.
The news highlights Microsoft's ongoing efforts to bridge the gap between Windows and Linux development environments.

stories.title: Mathematicians warn of AI threats to profession as industry encroaches
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: Mathematicians have issued warnings regarding the increasing influence of the tech industry on the field of mathematics.
The International Mathematical Union (IMU) has formally endorsed these concerns about industry encroachment.
The article discusses potential threats to the mathematical profession stemming from rapid technological advancements and industry demands.

stories.title: Imaginative Perception Tokens Enhance Spatial Reasoning in Multimodal Language Models
stories.author: Ranjay Krishna
bullet_points: The research addresses a known limitation in Vision Language Models (VLMs): poor spatial reasoning when information is not directly observable.
The core contribution is the introduction of 'Imaginative Perception Tokens' (IPT), which act as intermediate perceptual representations.
IPTs help VLMs perform complex spatial tasks, such as inferring unseen viewpoints or tracing paths through occluded areas.

stories.title: Quantifying Faithful Confidence Expression in Large Reasoning Models
stories.author: Arman Cohan
bullet_points: The paper addresses the critical issue of Faithful Confidence (FC) in Large Reasoning Models (LRMs).
FC refers to the alignment between a model's actual uncertainty and the confidence it linguistically expresses.
Accurate uncertainty communication is vital for building user trust in complex LLMs.

stories.title: QUBRIC: Co-Designing Queries and Rubrics for RL Beyond Verifiable Rewards
stories.author: Chao Zhang
bullet_points: Introduces QUBRIC, a new framework for Rubric-based Reinforcement Learning (RL).
Addresses a key limitation where existing methods fix the query distribution while optimizing rubrics.
Proposes co-designing queries and rubrics to overcome the issue of vague or unverifiable rewards in open-ended RL tasks.

stories.title: AlignAtt4LLM: Fast AlignAtt for Decoder-Only LLMs at IWSLT 2026 Simultaneous Speech Translation Task
stories.author: Dominik Macháček
bullet_points: Introduces AlignAtt4LLM, a new system for simultaneous speech translation (English to German, Italian, Chinese) targeting the IWSLT 2026 task.
The system utilizes a synchronous cascade, combining Qwen3-ASR for incremental transcript generation and Gemma-4 E4B-it for translation.
It marks a novel application of AlignAtt to decoder-only LLMs, adapting the technique to environments lacking traditional encoder-decoder cross-attention.

stories.title: Self-Refining Agentic Reinforcement Learning for Vision-Conditioned UAV Navigation
stories.author: Dzmitry Tsetserukou
bullet_points: Introduces AgenticRL, a framework designed to enhance autonomy in reinforcement learning for robotics.
Addresses a key limitation in current deep RL: heavy reliance on manually designed and tuned reward functions.
Focuses on applying this self-refining approach to complex tasks like vision-conditioned navigation for UAVs (drones).

stories.title: Using Reward Uncertainty to Induce Diverse Behaviour in Reinforcement Learning
stories.author: Mark Rowland
bullet_points: The paper addresses a limitation in classical Reinforcement Learning (RL), which typically favors deterministic policies.
It proposes using 'Reward Uncertainty' as a novel mechanism to naturally induce diverse behavior in RL agents.
This approach aims to overcome limitations of existing diversity methods (like entropy regularization) that often involve fragile trade-offs.

stories.title: Neuron Populations Exhibit Divergent Selectivity with Scale
stories.author: Yossi Gandelsman
bullet_points: The research investigates how neuron population selectivity changes as neural network scale increases, moving beyond traditional loss metrics.
The study utilizes 'Rosetta Neurons,' a specific, consistently behaving class of neurons across different models.
Observations were made in both large language models (up to 30B parameters) and vision models (up to 5B parameters).

stories.title: Skill-RM: Unifying Heterogeneous Evaluation Criteria via Agent Skill
stories.author: Guanjun Jiang
bullet_points: The paper addresses the challenge of integrating diverse evaluation criteria (rule-based, ground-truth, rubrics) used in LLM reward modeling.
It introduces Skill Reward Model (Skill-RM), a unified framework designed to consolidate heterogeneous feedback signals for LLM post-training.
This work is relevant to Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF) and advanced LLM fine-tuning pipelines.

stories.title: Quantifying Faithful Confidence Expression in Large Reasoning Models
stories.author: Arman Cohan
bullet_points: The research addresses the critical issue of 'faithful calibration' (FC) in Large Reasoning Models (LRMs).
FC refers to the alignment between a model's actual uncertainty and the confidence it linguistically expresses.
This topic is vital for LLM trustworthiness, as users often interpret reasoning traces as evidence of competence and confidence.

stories.title: QUBRIC: Co-Designing Queries and Rubrics for RL Beyond Verifiable Rewards
stories.author: Chao Zhang
bullet_points: Introduces QUBRIC, a framework addressing limitations in rubric-based Reinforcement Learning (RL).
Identifies a key bottleneck: rubric quality is constrained by the structure of the input queries.
Proposes co-designing queries and rubrics to move RL beyond reliance on strictly verifiable rewards.

stories.title: AlignAtt4LLM: Fast AlignAtt for Decoder-Only LLMs at IWSLT 2026 Simultaneous Speech Translation Task
stories.author: Dominik Macháček
bullet_points: Introduces AlignAtt4LLM, a new system for simultaneous speech translation (English to German, Italian, Chinese) for the IWSLT 2026 task.
The system uses a synchronous cascade involving Qwen3-ASR for transcript generation and Gemma-4 E4B-it for translation.
It marks a notable application of AlignAtt to decoder-only LLMs, adapting the technique without traditional encoder-decoder cross-attention.

stories.title: Efficient ASR Training with Conversations that Never Happened
stories.author: Péter Mihajlik
bullet_points: Addresses the data scarcity problem in Conversational Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR) for low-resource languages and niche domains.
Proposes an augmentation pipeline that synthesizes realistic, scenario-level dialogues using LLMs and TTS.
Evaluates different LLM families under various training budget and scaling strategies using the FastConformer-L model.

stories.title: Value-Aware Stochastic KV Cache Eviction for Reasoning Models
stories.author: Robin Jia
bullet_points: The paper addresses the memory and compute bottleneck in large reasoning models caused by long outputs.
It proposes a 'Value-Aware Stochastic KV Cache Eviction' method to manage the KV cache efficiently.
The research identifies that abnormally large magnitudes in a small fraction of value states are crucial factors for accurate eviction.

stories.title: Run AI Coding Agents Safely with Docker Sandboxes
stories.author: pradumnasaraf
bullet_points: The article addresses the security risks associated with running AI coding agents.
It proposes using Docker sandboxes as a method to safely execute these agents.
The core problem involves agents potentially running commands or downloading files from untrusted sources.
